239.reason: [When encountering a single '&', it should report an error and not return a valid AND token; also, accessing string.charAt(pos) may cause IndexOutOfBoundsException if pos is at the end of input]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.5 , reason:  [When encountering a single '&', it should report an error and not return a valid AND token; also, accessing string.charAt(pos) may cause IndexOutOfBoundsException if pos is at the end of input]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.5 , reason:  [When encountering a single '&', it should report an error and not return a valid AND token; also, accessing string.charAt(pos) may cause IndexOutOfBoundsException if pos is at the end of input]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.5 , reason:  [When encountering a single '&', it should not return a token of type AND ("&&"); instead, it should report an error and not produce a valid token. Returning a fabricated "&&" token is incorrect.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.5 , reason:  [When encountering a single '&', it should report an error and not return a valid AND token. Returning "&&" as the token string is incorrect because the actual input was only "&". Additionally, the logic for checking the next character does not guard against end-of-input, which may cause IndexOutOfBoundsException.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.5 , reason:  [When encountering a single '&', it should report an error and not return a valid AND token. Returning "&&" as the token string is incorrect because the actual input was only "&".]

250.reason: [When encountering a single '|', it should report an error and not return a valid OR token; also, accessing string.charAt(pos) may cause IndexOutOfBoundsException if pos is at the end of input]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.5 , reason:  [When encountering a single '|', it should report an error and not return a valid OR token; also, accessing string.charAt(pos) may cause IndexOutOfBoundsException if pos is at the end of input]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.5 , reason:  [When encountering a single '|', it should report an error and not return a valid OR token; also, accessing string.charAt(pos) may cause IndexOutOfBoundsException if pos is at the end of input]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.5 , reason:  [Similarly, a single '|' should not result in an OR ("||") token. The lexer must not generate a valid token for an invalid operator; it should only record the error and skip or handle appropriately without emitting a misleading token.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.5 , reason:  [Same issue as above: for a single '|', it incorrectly returns a token with value "||" instead of reflecting the actual erroneous input, and lacks bounds check before accessing string.charAt(pos).]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.5 , reason:  [When encountering a single '|', it should report an error and not return a valid OR token. Returning "||" as the token string is incorrect because the actual input was only "|".]
